behavior: Perches and watches for prey, then swoops down; also soars and circles with wings and tail spread.
field_marks: Medium-sized, slim-bodied hawk with a relatively long, banded tail.
Adults show warm rufous to reddish barring across the underparts.
Upperwings show conspicuous dark-and-pale checkering, with rufous shoulder coverts.
Tail is dark with narrow pale bands; immature birds are browner and more heavily streaked below.
Broad, rounded wings show small translucent pale crescents near the wingtips in flight.
Hooked dark bill with a yellow cere and yellow unfeathered legs.
habitat: Mature deciduous or mixed woodland, especially wet forest, bottomland woods, swamps, and wooded waterways; also uses open-canopy woodland edges and some wooded residential areas.
